published_prior_art.cite: YadlowskyNamkoongBasuDuchiTian2022: Lemma 2.1 and Theorem 2.1 give unbounded-outcome CATE bounds under finite conditional first moment and a likelihood-ratio-to-latent-law converse.
KallusMaoZhou2019: bounded-outcome MSM CATE interval estimator and sharpness; this is only the published bounded corner.
AronowLee2013 + MiratrixWagerZubizarreta2018: support/shape-constrained population mean bounds; proposal's tail-budget comparison is not well-posed as stated.
Tan2006: Gamma=1 is the no-hidden-bias point-identification corner.
KallusMaoZhou2019: bounded-outcome limit is the original bounded MSM CATE setting.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: YadlowskyNamkoongBasuDuchiTian2022 already treats unbounded CATE bounds under Gamma-selection with E[|Y(z)| | X] < infinity and gives the likelihood-ratio sharp program plus latent-law converse; finite alpha>1 is stronger, not a new regime.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The proposal says YadlowskyNamkoongBasuDuchiTian2022 requires uniformly bounded outcomes, but their Theorem 2.1 assumes only finite conditional first moments for CATE bounds; higher moments enter later for inference.

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The bounded-outcome/KMZ recovery theorem is a restatement of the published bounded-support corner, not a novel theorem.

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 1
novelty_flags.one_line: KallusMaoZhou2019 is cited as Theorem 3.1, eq. (7), but the AISTATS paper's relevant bounded-outcome sharpness statement is Theorem 1; eq. (7) is the CATE bound definition, not the claimed theorem.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: AronowLee2013 gives sharp population-mean intervals under bounded selection probabilities and bounded outcomes; it does not define the proposal's support-cap cone T_infty or an alpha-to-infinity tail-budget frontier.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: Definition 1 defines (U*,Y*) but not the treatment-assignment kernel or full latent extension needed to reproduce P(Y|A=1,X=x) and attain mu_1^U.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: Exhibit 9.1 Step 3 is algebraically false: the defined mixture has E[Y*]=E_F[Y]=1.5, not the claimed mu_1^U≈1.6659.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The abstract promises a moment-balanced threshold y_alpha^*(x), but Section 6 defines y_Gamma^*(x) as a Gamma/(Gamma+1)-quantile independent of alpha.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The cone comparison is reversed/ill-defined: T_infty={support within [-M^(1/alpha),M^(1/alpha)]} is contained in T_alpha, while the conjecture asserts T_alpha subsetneq T_infty.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: Exhibit 9.1 uses Uniform[0,3] as the support-cap competitor when M_2=3, but that law is not supported in [-sqrt(3),sqrt(3)] as Section 6 defines T_infty.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Pivot the published comparison around Yadlowsky et al. 2022 as the true closest prior art, and state a strictly stronger object they do not provide, if one exists.
Replace the current (U*,Y*) definition with a full latent law including A|U,X and verify it reproduces the observed treated law and the counterfactual reweighted law.
Repair or drop Conjecture 2: redefine the tail-budget/support-cap feasible sets so the inclusion direction and worked example are internally consistent.
Use exact location anchors from the closest papers: Yadlowsky Lemma 2.1/Theorem 2.1, KMZ Theorem 1/Proposition 1, Asiaee et al. Section 6 limitations.

recommended_next_step: Reject this kernel and redraft against Yadlowsky et al. 2022; the current flagship novelty claim is already published and the exhibited coupling fails its own sanity check.
